title: Statutory silence vs constitutional imperative: Supreme Court ruling on tribunal composition
description: On March 10, 2026, a two-judge bench of the Supreme Court in Pannalal Bhansali v. Bharti Telecom Ltd used parliament's silence in the Companies Act, 2013 to permit a technical member majority National Company Law Appellate Tribunal (NCLAT) bench - the identical silence that a Constitution Bench used in 2010 to prohibit exactly that.The logical inversion is complete. The constitutional damage it causes is structural and, if left uncorrected, permanent.A constitutional safeguard imposed by a five-
